pattern: A single greedy pass plants a flower in every empty plot whose two neighbours are also empty, which is optimal because planting as early as possible never blocks a later opportunity.
hints: Scan the plots and decide each one locally: a plot can hold a flower only if it and both immediate neighbours are empty.
Treating the area just outside the bed as empty lets the endpoints be handled the same way as interior plots.
Walk left to right; when a zero has empty (or absent) neighbours on both sides, set it to one and count it, then check whether the count reached n.

edge_inputs.description: gap of three empties fits one flower
same bed cannot fit two flowers
all empty long bed packs alternate plots
leading and trailing empties at the ends

stress.description: random valid beds with no adjacent ones, plus a random target
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    bed = []
    prev = 0
    for _ in range(n):
        if prev == 1:
            bed.append(0)
            prev = 0
        else:
            v = rng.randrange(0, 2)
            bed.append(v)
            prev = v
    target = rng.randrange(0, n + 1)
    return [bed, target]
